query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "full attention strikes back: transferring full attention into sparse w" linguistics
rationale: The paper focuses on sequential recommendation systems and user behavior modeling, which is an off-domain application compared to the user's question about transferring full attention mechanisms to sparse attention in the field of linguistics. While both involve "attention mechanisms," the paper does not address the specific mechanism of attention sparsification, the linguistic domain, or the "Full Attention Strikes Back" context, failing to meet the criteria for same-mechanism evidence or cross
